name: Avoid Negative Conditionals
source: Clean Code by Robert C. Martin, Chapter 17: Smells and Heuristics (G29)
problem: Negated boolean expressions require readers to perform a mental double-negation, slowing comprehension and increasing the chance of logic errors.
steps: Find any negated boolean condition: !isNotX(), !isEmpty(), if (!condition).
Rename the method or variable to its positive form, or invert the logic of the predicate.
Adjust call sites to use the positive form directly.
Where a positive name is not possible, negate once at the call site rather than nesting negations.
Prefer positive assertions in tests (assertTrue/assertFalse over complex negated conditions).
